common.follow_us: Follow us on social media for updates on when the District opens its gates.

home.title: The District // Shimokitan
home.description: The digital back-alleys of Japanese culture. Archive anime, music, and games as lived memories-not data points.
home.tagline: An archive of lived culture, not data points.

contact.title: ESTABLISH CONNECTION
contact.description: Have a question, feedback, or just want to say hello? Open a channel and reach out to the Shimokitan team.
contact.transmission_sent_description: Your message has been successfully encrypted and delivered to our secure servers.
contact.error_toast: Failed to send transmission. Please try again.

about.title: About Shimokitan
about.a1_1: Shimokitan is a curated archive for Japanese creative culture. We index, organize, and celebrate anime, music, games, and the creators behind them - not as data points or rankings, but as lived experiences worth remembering.
about.a1_2: Think of it as a personal record collection for everything you care about in Japanese media. Each entry is an artifact - a piece of culture documented with the attention it deserves.
about.a2_1: The internet has an abundance of databases and trackers for anime, music, and games. Most of them treat culture as spreadsheets - scores, stats, completion percentages. We wanted something different.
about.a2_2: Shimokitan is built on the idea that your taste tells a story. The anime that kept you up at night, the album you played on repeat during a specific season, the game that changed how you see things - these are memories, not metrics. We archive them with that weight.
about.a3_1: Shimokitan is a curated archive built on consent. We do not archive recording artists or specific creators without their explicit permission. We invite them to reside in the District, and if they agree, they choose how they want to be presented: via third-party embeds or hosted directly in our custom, distraction-free players.
about.a3_2: Because we operate on a strictly consent-first basis for artists, every creator you see here has agreed to be here. For broader architectural entries like anime or video games, we index public metadata and host visual assets (like covers) purely for referential and archival purposes.
about.artifacts_desc: The core entries. Anime series, albums, films, games, visual novels. Each has metadata, a cover, embedded media, and editorial context.
about.entities_desc: The people and studios behind the work. Directors, composers, animators, labels, development teams.
about.collections_desc: Curated groupings organized around a theme, a moment, or a mood.
about.zines_desc: Community logs and radio signals. Written reflections on artifacts and District events.
about.a4_1: Shimokitan is independently operated from Indonesia. There is no company, no investors, no board. It exists because the people building it genuinely love this culture and wanted a better home for it online.
about.a4_2: The platform is named after Shimokitazawa - a neighborhood in Tokyo known for its independent record shops, second-hand stores, and small theaters. The kind of place where you discover things by wandering, guided by human resonance rather than predictive algorithms.
about.a4_3: The platform is built by a solo developer using AI coding assistants. While the code is assisted, the culture we archive is strictly human - we do not index AI-generated media.
about.a5_1: Shimokitan is built for anyone who shares a deep appreciation for Japanese creative work, regardless of where they are. The platform is free to browse, free to read, and free to explore.
about.a5_2: Everything you see on the platform is hand-curated. There are no engagement-driven feeds, no auto-generated pages, and no user-submitted content at this time. Discovery here is deterministic, never a black box.
about.a6_1: If you have questions, suggestions, or just want to say hello, reach out through our contact page. You can also find us on X and Instagram.
about.legal_notice_content_1: All anime, game, music titles, artwork, and related trademarks are property of their respective owners. Shimokitan claims no ownership over indexed content. For details on intellectual property, content usage, and affiliate practices, see our Legal Documents.
about.legal_notice_content_2: While we strive for accuracy, we cannot guarantee the completeness or correctness of all metadata. Information on the platform is provided for reference and archival purposes only.
about.vision_disclaimer: This roadmap represents our current vision and may change at any time without notice. Nothing here constitutes a commitment or guarantee of future features.

section1.title: Where We Are Going
section1.content: Shimokitan started as a personal archive - a place to catalog the anime, music, and games that matter. The long-term vision is to grow it into a living cultural index that anyone can contribute to, without losing the curatorial soul that makes it different from a list or a tracker.

section2.title: Phase 0: Prelude
section2.content: This is where we are now. The District gates are currently closed. We are actively curating the space to gather our first few artifacts before the platform opens to the public.
section2.l1: Initial launch of the coming-soon page.
section2.l2: Gathering and documenting the first set of cultural artifacts.

section3.title: Phase 1: Foundation
section3.content: When the gates open, the core archive will go live, featuring hand-curated artifacts, entities, and collections. All content will be managed internally. The primary focus is data structure, editorial quality, and the browsing experience.
section3.l1: Core artifact database with metadata, resources, and credits.
section3.l2: Entity profiles for creators, studios, and labels.
section3.l3: Curated collections organized by theme and mood.
section3.l4: Official media embeds and links to streaming platforms.

section4.title: Phase 2: Community & Resonance
section4.content: The opening of the District gates introduces the Resonance Engine. When we open the platform to community participation, we refuse to use engagement feeds or algorithms that prioritize the influencer. Instead, visibility is determined by collective, verified human depth.
section4.l1: Weighted Zines — Users contribute Zines instead of "likes." Zines carry "Heat" based on the author's earned reputation, defending the archive against bots and paid manipulation.
section4.l2: Deterministic Discovery — An artifact's visibility is the sum of the "Heat" from all its attached Zines. High-integrity human contributions move an artifact into the light.
section4.l3: Personal Shelves & Profiles — Tools for organizing your own artifact collection and building your curatorial reputation.

section5.title: Revenue Model
section5.content: Shimokitan currently operates without monetization. If revenue becomes necessary, potential options include affiliate partnerships with streaming services, premium features, voluntary support, or merchandise. Any monetization decisions will be made with transparency and community awareness.

section6.title: What We Will Not Do
section6.content: Some things are intentionally out of scope for Shimokitan:
section6.l1: We will not archive artists without their consent. We prioritize the quality of consent over the quantity of content.
section6.l2: We will not implement engagement-driven feeds. We operate a Zero-Prediction Policy—we will never 'guess' what you want to see based on watch time or scroll depth. Discovery should be intentional, not dopamine-driven.
section6.l3: We will not sell user data or implement intrusive advertising.
section6.l4: We will not chase scale at the expense of quality. Growth should follow curation, not the other way around.
section6.l5: We will not index or accept AI-generated media. Shimokitan is strictly an archive for human effort, human creators, and human culture.

legal.help_text: If you have questions about our policies, please contact our legal team.

s1.title: 1. Who We Are
s1.content: Shimokitan ("we", "us", "our") operates an independently run platform (the "Service") based in Indonesia. The Service serves as a curated archival index for Japanese creative culture, including anime, music, games, and related media. Shimokitan is operated by an individual, not a registered company.

s2.title: 2. What We Collect
s2.p1: We are committed to collecting as little personal data as possible. Shimokitan does **not** offer user accounts, sign-up, or sign-in functionality. We do not track, profile, or fingerprint visitors.
s2.p2: We process personal data through two voluntary submission points:
s2.l1: **Contact Form**: We receive your name, email, and message via email (Resend). This data is not stored in a database and is deleted after resolution.
s2.l2: **Artist Registry Form**: We collect artist names, professional titles, social links, creative samples, and contact emails. To prevent spam and maintain the integrity of our curation queue, we also record the **IP address** of the sender. This data is stored securely in our database (Neon) for manual review by the architects.
s2.p3: We process this data on the basis of your explicit consent provided at the time of transmission.

s2_5.title: 2.5 Artist and Rights Holder Communications
s2_5.content: When artists, creators, or rights holders grant us permission to feature their work, we retain copies of permission correspondence (emails, messages, and related documentation) in a private, secure archive. This documentation is stored for verification, legal compliance, and record-keeping purposes.
s2_5.l2: Permission grants and any specified conditions
s2_5.p2: This information is **not** shared publicly, sold, or used for any purpose other than verifying authorized use of their work on Shimokitan. Permission documentation is retained indefinitely unless the artist or rights holder requests deletion of their information. To request removal of your permission documentation, contact us at legal@shimokitan.live.

s3.title: 3. Cookies and Tracking
s3.p1: We do not use analytics cookies or tracking scripts. The cookies that may be present on our site are:
s3.l1: Essential infrastructure cookies set by Cloudflare (our hosting provider) for security and performance purposes, such as bot detection.
s3.l2: Third-party cookies set by YouTube when embedded video content is loaded on artifact pages. These cookies are governed by Google's Privacy Policy.
s3.p2: For more details, see our Cookie Policy.

s4.title: 4. Third-Party Services
s4.p1: We use the following third-party services that may process data in connection with your visit:
s4.l1: **Cloudflare** (United States) - Hosting, CDN, and DDoS protection.
s4.l2: **Resend** (United States) - Email delivery for contact form submissions.
s4.l3: **Google** (United States) - Embedded video playback (YouTube) and operational email forwarding (Gmail).
s4.l4: **Hostinger** (International) - Email hosting for contact form submissions.
s4.p2: Our database infrastructure is provided by **Neon** (United States); no personal visitor data is stored in our database. We plan to integrate affiliate programs (Crunchyroll, Apple Music) that will use tracking parameters. See our [Affiliate Disclosure](/affiliate-disclosure) for details.

s5.title: 5. International Data Transfers
s5.content: Our infrastructure partners (Cloudflare, Resend) are based in the United States. By using our Service, data relating to your requests may be processed outside of your home country. We rely on the privacy safeguards provided by these service providers, including their data processing agreements and, where applicable, Standard Contractual Clauses or equivalent transfer mechanisms recognized under applicable data protection law.

s6.title: 6. Data Retention
s6.content: Contact form submissions are processed in real-time via email and are **not stored** in any database. Emails received through the contact form are retained in our mailbox only until the inquiry is resolved, after which they are deleted. Infrastructure logs are retained by Cloudflare in accordance with their data retention practices and our account configuration.

s7.title: 7. Your Rights
s7.p1: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:
s7.l1: Request access to any personal data we hold about you.
s7.l2: Request correction or deletion of your personal data.
s7.l3: Object to or restrict our processing of your data.
s7.l4: Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority in your jurisdiction.
s7.p2: Since we do not maintain user accounts or databases of personal data at this time, most of these rights have limited practical applicability.

s8.title: 8. Children
s8.content: Shimokitan is not directed at individuals under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us.

s9.title: 9. Changes to This Policy
s9.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make material changes, we will make reasonable efforts to provide notice, such as displaying a prominent announcement on the Service. The "Last Updated" date at the top of this page reflects the most recent revision. Continued use of the Service after changes have been posted constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

s10.title: 10. Contact
s10.content: For privacy-related inquiries, contact us at privacy@shimokitan.live.

s1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
s1.content: By accessing or using Shimokitan (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please discontinue use of the Service immediately.

s2.title: 2. About the Service
s2.content: Shimokitan is an independently operated, curated archival index for Japanese creative culture. The Service is operated by an individual based in Indonesia. At this time, Shimokitan does not offer user accounts or user-generated content features.

s3.title: 3. Use of the Service
s3.p1: You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You must not:
s3.l1: Interfere with or disrupt the Service or its infrastructure.
s3.l2: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any portion of the Service.
s3.l3: Use the Service to distribute malware, spam, or harmful content.
s3.l4: Scrape, crawl, or harvest content from the Service in bulk.
s3.l5: Misrepresent your identity or affiliation when contacting us.

s4.title: 4. Intellectual Property
s4.p1: All original content provided on the Service - including but not limited to editorial text, UI/UX design, graphics, logos, and the "District" concept framework - is the property of Shimokitan and is protected by applicable copyright and intellectual property laws.
s4.p2: Third-party content (including anime titles, music, video embeds, and artist imagery) remains the property of its respective owners. Shimokitan does not claim ownership of third-party content indexed or embedded within the Service.

s5.title: 5. Third-Party Content and Embeds
s5.content: The Service embeds content from third-party platforms, including YouTube. By using the Service, you also agree to the terms of these third-party providers. We are not responsible for the content, availability, or policies of external platforms.

s6.title: 6. Affiliate Links
s6.content: The Service may contain affiliate links to third-party platforms such as Crunchyroll and Apple Music. We may earn a commission from qualifying actions at no additional cost to you. For more information, see our Affiliate Disclosure.

s7.title: 7. Disclaimer of Warranties
s7.content: The Service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied. We do not warrant that the Service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or free of harmful components.

s8.title: 8. Limitation of Liability
s8.content: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Shimokitan shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits, data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses arising from your use of or inability to use the Service.

s9.title: 9. Termination
s9.content: We reserve the right to restrict or terminate access to the Service at our discretion, without prior notice, for conduct that we believe violates these Terms or is harmful to the Service, other users, or third parties.

s10.title: 10. Modifications
s10.content: We may revise these Terms at any time by updating this page. When we make material changes, we will make reasonable efforts to provide notice, such as displaying a prominent announcement on the Service. The "Last Updated" date at the top reflects the most recent revision. Continued use of the Service after changes have been posted constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

s11.title: 11. Eligibility
s11.content: The Service is not directed at individuals under the age of 13. By using the Service, you represent that you are at least 13 years of age. If you are under 13, you must discontinue use of the Service immediately.

s12.title: 12. Indemnification
s12.content: You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Shimokitan and its operator from any claims, damages, losses, or expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising from your violation of these Terms, your use of the Service, or your infringement of any third-party rights.

s13.title: 13. Governing Law
s13.content: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Indonesia. Any disputes arising from or relating to these Terms or your use of the Service shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Republic of Indonesia.

s14.title: 14. Contact
s14.content: If you have questions about these Terms, contact us at legal@shimokitan.live.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.description: This Cookie Policy explains how Shimokitan uses cookies and similar technologies when you visit our website.

s1.title: 1. What are Cookies?
s1.content: Cookies are small data files placed on your device when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites function properly, enhance performance, and provide information to site operators.

s2.title: 2. Cookies We Use
s2.content: Shimokitan uses a minimal set of cookies. We do **not** use analytics cookies, advertising cookies, or tracking pixels.

s3.title: 3. Essential / Infrastructure Cookies
s3.content: **Cloudflare cookies** (e.g., __cf_bm, cf_clearance) - Set by our hosting and CDN provider, Cloudflare, for bot detection, DDoS protection, and security purposes. These are strictly necessary for the secure operation of the site. You cannot opt out of these without leaving the site.

s4.title: 4. Third-Party Cookies
s4.p1: **YouTube / Google cookies** - When you view a page containing an embedded YouTube video, Google may set cookies on your device (such as VISITOR_INFO1_LIVE, YSC, and others). These cookies are governed by Google's Privacy Policy. We do not control the data collected by these cookies.
s4.p2: If additional third-party integrations are introduced in the future, this policy will be updated accordingly.

s5.title: 5. How to Control Cookies
s5.p1: You can control and manage cookies through your browser settings:
s5.l1: **Chrome**: Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies and other site data
s5.l2: **Firefox**: Settings → Privacy & Security → Cookies and Site Data
s5.l5: You can also **block third-party cookies** to prevent YouTube cookies while still accessing the site.
s5.l6: Use browser extensions like **uBlock Origin** to selectively block third-party content.
s5.p2: Please note that blocking essential Cloudflare cookies may prevent the site from loading properly.

s6.title: 6. Contact
s6.content: If you have questions about our use of cookies, contact us at privacy@shimokitan.live.

s1.title: 1. Our Approach to Content
s1.content: Shimokitan operates on a strict consent-first policy for recording artists and specific creators. We do not catalog or archive artists without their explicit permission. For generalized entries like anime and video games, we act as a curated archival index using publicly available metadata.

s2.title: 2. Consent-First Hosting
s2.p1: When an artist agrees to be archived, they may choose how their media is presented: via third-party embeds (like YouTube) or hosted directly on our infrastructure. Any playable media hosted by us is done so with explicit, documented consent from the rights holder.
s2.p2: We do not host, upload, or distribute playable copyrighted media without this explicit permission. If an artist chooses only to be embedded, we rely exclusively on embedding from platforms whose terms permit it.

s3.title: 3. Visual Media and Referential Use
s3.content: For broader archival entries (anime, games), thumbnails and cover images are hosted on our infrastructure purely for contextual, referential, and archival purposes. We make no claim of ownership over these assets. For artists who grant us permission, hosting their avatar/profile imagery is a requirement for residency in the District.

s4.title: 4. Visual Media Attribution
s4.content: All visual assets displayed on Shimokitan include source attribution where available. Because every artist profile signifies a mutual agreement and established consent, verified badges or indicators are not utilized or necessary on the platform.

s5.title: 5. Original Content
s5.content: All original editorial content, UI design, graphics, and the "District" concept framework on Shimokitan are protected by copyright. Reproduction without written permission is prohibited.

s6.title: 6. Our Approach to Rights
s6.content: Shimokitan's approach is designed to respect rights holders:
s6.l1: We embed content from platforms that authorize external embedding (per their Terms of Service)
s6.l2: We provide attribution and link directly to official distribution channels
s6.l3: We do not host or redistribute playable copyrighted media files
s6.l4: We respond promptly to rights holder concerns
s6.p2: If you are a rights holder and have questions about how we reference your work, please contact us at **legal@shimokitan.live**.

s7.title: 7. Visitor Responsibility
s7.content: Shimokitan does not provide legal advice regarding copyright status, fair use, or licensing of indexed content. Visitors are responsible for their own use of linked materials.

s8.title: 8. Content Removal Requests
s8.p1: If you are a copyright owner or authorized agent and believe that material on Shimokitan infringes your copyright, you may submit a removal request. Please include the following information:
s8.l2: Identification of the material on Shimokitan (e.g., a URL).
s8.l4: A statement that you have a good-faith belief that the use of the material is not authorized by the copyright owner, its agent, or the law.
s8.l5: A statement, made under declaration of truthfulness and subject to legal liability for false claims, that the information in your notification is accurate and that you are the copyright owner or authorized to act on behalf of the copyright owner.

s9.title: 9. Counter-Notification
s9.content: If you believe that material was removed or restricted in error, you may submit a counter-notification to legal@shimokitan.live. Your counter-notification should include: (1) identification of the material that was removed and its location before removal; (2) a statement, under declaration of truthfulness, that you have a good-faith belief the material was removed or restricted as a result of mistake or misidentification; (3) your name, address, and contact information; (4) a statement consenting to the jurisdiction of the courts in your place of residence (or the Republic of Indonesia if you reside outside Indonesia); and (5) your physical or electronic signature.

s10.title: 10. Voluntary Content Review
s10.content: Shimokitan periodically reviews indexed content and may proactively remove or restrict access to materials that we reasonably believe to infringe on third-party rights or violate our content policies.

s11.title: 11. Repeat Infringers
s11.content: Shimokitan reserves the right to delist content from sources that repeatedly generate valid infringement claims.

s12.title: 12. Contact
s12.content: For copyright inquiries, contact us at legal@shimokitan.live.

s1.title: 1. Overview
s1.content: Shimokitan will participate in affiliate programs with third-party platforms once they are implemented. When active, some links on our site will be "affiliate links."

s2.title: 2. Planned Affiliate Partners
s2.p1: We plan to participate in affiliate programs with:
s2.l2: **Apple Music** - Music streaming subscriptions and purchases
s2.p2: This disclosure will be updated when affiliate links go live.

s3.title: 3. How Affiliate Links Work
s3.content: Affiliate links contain tracking parameters that allow the merchant to attribute a referral to Shimokitan. These tracking parameters may result in the merchant setting cookies on your device.

s4.title: 4. Editorial Independence
s4.content: Affiliate relationships do **not** influence our editorial decisions. Our curation, editorial commentary, and archival decisions are independent of any affiliate partnership.

s5.title: 5. Identifying Affiliate Links
s5.content: Pages or sections of the site that contain affiliate links will be identified with a visible indicator, such as a "Partner Link" label.

s6.title: 6. Contact
s6.content: If you have questions about our affiliate relationships, contact us at partners@shimokitan.live.

community.title: Community Guidelines
community.description:
Shimokitan does not currently offer user accounts or community features.

When we introduce these features in the future, we'll establish clear guidelines for respectful participation, content standards, and prohibited conduct. Until then, if you have feedback or concerns, please contact us via our [Contact Page](/contact).

s1.title: General
s1.a1: Shimokitan is a curated archive for Japanese creative culture. We index, organize, and celebrate anime, music, games, and the creators behind them - not as data points or rankings, but as lived experiences worth remembering.

s2.title: Using the Platform
s2.q1: Do you host illegal or pirated content?
s2.a1: No. Shimokitan operates on a consent-first policy for recording artists. We do not host playable copyrighted media without explicit permission from the rights holder. Visual assets for broader entries (anime, games) are hosted purely for referential and archival purposes. Otherwise, we rely entirely on external embedding from authorized sources.

s3.title: Accounts & Community
s3.a1: Not currently. Shimokitan does not offer user accounts or community features at this time, but we plan to introduce them in the future.

s4.title: Contact
s4.q1: How can I contact the team?
s4.a1: You can reach out through our [Contact Page](/contact) or email us at legal@shimokitan.live for legal questions.

registry.title: ARTIST_REGISTRY_PROTOCOLS
registry.description: Establish your residency in the Shimokitan district. We manually curate every signal to ensure high-resonance artifacts.
registry.step_01_desc: Artist name, classification, and professional role.
registry.step_02_desc: External communication endpoints. Please open your notifications so we can send you a message.
registry.step_03_desc: Digital samples of your creative output. At least one required.
registry.step_04_desc: Final submission. Please ensure your email is open so we can send you a message.
registry.form_why: Why do you want us to archive you? (Short application)
registry.data_collection_warning: We collect your name, email, and creative samples for curation purposes. Access is limited by IP address (1 submission per 24 hours) to prevent spam. By clicking submit, you agree to our data handling protocols as outlined in our {{privacy_link}}.
registry.success_desc: Your residency application has been successfully encrypted and queued for manual curation by the architects.
registry.wait_period: Please allow 7-14 solar cycles for manual frequency alignment.
registry.error_rate_limit: SIGNAL_OVERLOAD: Transmission window locked for 24h. Your frequency has already been recorded.
